name: Claude Agent SDK
description: SDK exposing Claude Code's production agent harness (tool loop, permission system, subagents, MCP) for building general-purpose agents in TypeScript and Python. Renamed from Claude Code SDK in September 2025.

evidence.value: Reuses the same battle-tested agent harness that powers Claude Code, inheriting its task-completion behavior

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Evaluation of agents built on the SDK harness across coding and non-coding tasks

evidence.value: Built-in file, bash, and web tools plus custom tools and MCP servers with a managed agentic tool loop

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Testing of built-in tool loop, custom tool definitions, and MCP server integration

evidence.value: Automatic context management and compaction enable long-horizon multi-step agent runs

multi_step_planning.methodology: Long-horizon agent task evaluation using the SDK's managed loop and compaction

evidence.value: Session persistence and resume, CLAUDE.md-style memory files, and automatic compaction of long contexts

memory_persistence.methodology: Review of session resume, memory file support, and context compaction behavior

evidence.value: Harness retries and self-corrects on tool failures; hooks allow custom failure handling

error_recovery.methodology: Observed recovery from tool errors and failed commands in SDK-built agents

evidence.value: First-class subagents with isolated contexts, custom prompts, and per-agent tool restrictions

agent_collaboration.methodology: Testing of programmatic subagent definition and parallel delegation

evidence.value: Inherits Claude Code's sandboxing support, including OS-level filesystem and network isolation for bash

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Review of inherited sandbox configuration and bash isolation options

evidence.value: Programmatic permission modes, allow/deny rules, canUseTool callbacks, and hooks for fine-grained control

access_control.methodology: Assessment of permission rules, programmatic approval callbacks, and hook-based gating

evidence.value: Permission gating and sandboxing mitigate injection impact, but defenses against untrusted content depend on developer configuration

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Review of documented mitigations and developer responsibility for untrusted input handling

evidence.value: Sessions and subagents maintain isolated contexts; deployment isolation is the integrator's responsibility

data_isolation.methodology: Architecture review of session/subagent context isolation in self-hosted deployments

evidence.value: Distributed under Anthropic Commercial Terms (proprietary); SDK wrapper code inspectable but the underlying harness is closed

open_source_transparency.methodology: License and source availability review of npm/PyPI packages

evidence.value: API traffic not used for training by default; agent state and transcripts stay on the integrator's infrastructure

data_retention.methodology: Review of Anthropic API retention terms and local-state architecture

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II and GDPR-aligned DPA available; Bedrock/Vertex routing supports regional data residency

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance certification review including cloud-provider routing options

evidence.value: Data flows only to the configured Claude endpoint (Anthropic API, Bedrock, or Vertex AI)

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis across supported model endpoints

evidence.value: Harness runs entirely on your infrastructure, but inference requires Claude via API, Bedrock, or Vertex; no local models

local_deployment_option.methodology: Deployment options assessment for self-hosted harness with cloud-only inference

evidence.value: Thorough TypeScript and Python guides covering permissions, subagents, MCP, sessions, and deployment

documentation_quality.methodology: Documentation completeness review across both language SDKs

evidence.value: Streaming message API surfaces every tool call and result; hooks enable custom audit logging

execution_traceability.methodology: Review of message stream observability and hook-based audit trails

evidence.value: Agent reasoning, tool intents, and permission requests are exposed in the streamed transcript

decision_explainability.methodology: Assessment of streamed reasoning visibility and permission-request context

evidence.value: Proprietary license (Anthropic Commercial Terms); package code visible but not open source

open_source_code.methodology: Open source assessment of SDK packages and underlying harness

evidence.value: High npm download volume, frequent releases, and growing ecosystem of SDK-built agents since the 2025-09-29 rename

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis via package downloads, release cadence, and ecosystem projects

evidence.value: Single package install for TypeScript or Python; minimal code to launch a fully tool-equipped agent

ease_of_integration.methodology: Integration complexity assessment from install to first working agent

evidence.value: Stateless harness instances scale horizontally; throughput bounded by Claude API rate limits

scalability.methodology: Assessment of horizontal scaling patterns and API rate limit constraints

evidence.value: Free SDK; costs are pay-as-you-go Claude tokens, which vary widely with agent autonomy and task length

Since 2026-06-15, Agent SDK and non-interactive (claude -p) usage on subscription plans draws from a separate monthly Agent SDK credit ($20 on Pro, $100 on Max 5x, $200 on Max 20x), capping subscription-based SDK spend

cost_predictability.methodology: Pricing model analysis of token-based costs for long-running agents, including the June 2026 subscription-credit change

evidence.value: Per-message usage/cost reporting and hooks for custom telemetry; full APM requires external tooling

monitoring_capabilities.methodology: Review of built-in usage reporting and integration points for external observability

evidence.value: Same harness running Claude Code in production at scale; renamed and generalized for agents on 2025-09-29

production_readiness.methodology: Maturity assessment based on shared production harness and release stability

code-generation.notes: Inherits Claude Code's coding harness; ideal for building custom coding agents and CI bots

research-assistant.notes: Strong general-agent harness for research workflows with web, file, and MCP tools

customer-support.notes: Permission system and subagents suit support automation; requires custom integration work

data-analysis.notes: Bash and file tools enable scripted analysis agents with auditable execution

best_for: Developers building production agents on Claude without writing their own tool loop
Teams extending Claude Code workflows into custom applications and services
Enterprises needing permissioned, auditable agents on Anthropic API, Bedrock, or Vertex
Builders who want subagents, hooks, and MCP out of the box
strengths: Production-proven harness shared with Claude Code (tool loop, compaction, retries)
Fine-grained permission system with allow/deny rules, callbacks, hooks, and sandboxing
First-class subagents and MCP support for composable agent systems
Both TypeScript and Python SDKs with feature parity
Automatic context management enables long-running agents
Runs on Anthropic API, Amazon Bedrock, or Google Vertex AI
limitations: Proprietary license (Anthropic Commercial Terms); not open source
Claude models only; no third-party or local model support
Token costs for autonomous agents can be hard to forecast
Heavier runtime footprint than thin LLM client libraries
Security posture depends on integrator configuring permissions and sandboxing correctly

tool_support: Built-in file, bash, and web tools
metadata.first_release: 2025 (as Claude Code SDK); renamed Claude Agent SDK 2025-09-29
metadata.pricing: Free SDK; pay-as-you-go Claude token costs via API, or monthly Agent SDK credits on subscription plans since 2026-06-15 ($20 Pro / $100 Max 5x / $200 Max 20x)
